title: The Model Sheet
author: Zachary Proser
description: How to read a language model like a spec sheet: parameters, dense versus MoE, base versus instruction-tuned versus abliterated, quantization, and the memory math that decides what runs locally — then how to test a model on your own task instead of trusting a benchmark.

blueprint.subject: MODEL SELECTION
blueprint.subtitle: A model's name hides the four numbers that decide whether it fits your machine, your task, and your budget.
rfiSuggestions: Dense or MoE for a 128GB machine?
Which quantization keeps enough quality for coding?
How do I test a model on my own task instead of trusting a leaderboard?
